Confirmed via xarray data inspection of the real NOAA netCDF file

Confirmed independently using xarray on the real downloaded NOAA netCDF dataset over bounding boxes for North/South Australian coasts. Calculated mean January precipitation for the North coast at ~8.2e-05 Kg/m^2/s and South at ~1.1e-05, and in August North at ~3.3e-06 and South at ~2.3e-05, confirming the wet/dry coast and fire seasonality dynamics.

privileged_info_summary: The data used for this task is the real-world NCEP Reanalysis Monthly Long Term Mean Precipitation Rate (1981-2010), downloaded directly from NOAA's public archive. The target dataset variable 'prate' was analyzed using Python's xarray. It confirmed the core climatic deduction logic specified in the task: in January, the North coast is statistically wetter than the South coast (8.2e-5 vs 1.1e-5 Kg/m^2/s), while in August, the South is wetter than the North (2.3e-5 vs 3.3e-6 Kg/m^2/s). This confirms that August is the driest (peak fire) season for Northern Australia and January is the driest (peak fire) season for Southern Australia. All file paths, expected deliverables, and task context variables exactly correspond to the internal setup scripts and verifier logic.
